summary: Drew DeVault's February 2021 status update covers SourceHut maintenance work and teases a secret project that turns out to be an early look at the Hare programming language. The post reveals a working cat implementation in Hare, showcasing its systems programming syntax with explicit error handling and C-like control flow. He mentions progress on todo.sr.ht's GraphQL API and an Alpine 3.13 upgrade. The post closes with a call for volunteers to help build out the standard library for the secret language project.
key_points: todo.sr.ht's GraphQL API is progressing toward a read-only release
An early reveal of Hare's syntax through a cat utility implementation, showing match-based error handling and explicit memory management patterns
SourceHut infrastructure was upgraded to Alpine 3.13 without issues
Recruiting systems programmers to help flesh out the standard library for the new language
money_quotes: I've been working hard on free software, what with having little else to do.
The rest of the progress was made in secret. Secret! You will have to live in ignorance for now.
I'm looking for a few volunteers to get involved and help flesh out the standard library.
key_insight: This post is a historical artifact marking one of the earliest public glimpses of the Hare programming language, revealed through a simple cat implementation that already demonstrates the language's philosophy of explicit error handling and systems-level control.
